prompt: A clean, realistic residential smart-home commissioning scene for an article about a Home Assistant Matter PASE timeout when another home platform can pair the same accessory. Warm lived-in reading nook with a natural oak side shelf; one compact unbranded Matter smart plug connected to a simple floor lamp at a normal wall outlet; a homeowner's hand holding a modern unbranded smartphone close to the plug as if retrying secure setup; a small fanless local home server and one neutral unbranded smart-home hub naturally on the shelf in the background. Photorealistic editorial interior photography, 3:2 landscape, medium-wide eye-level view, soft late-afternoon window light plus warm lamp glow, warm cream, oak, and off-white with restrained dark-green accents. Phone and smart plug recognizable in the central card crop; local server and separate hub visible but secondary. Physically plausible outlet, cables, ports, hand anatomy, and device scale; all hardware unbranded; no legible screen UI; no text, logos, trademarks, watermark, headline, readable QR code, charts, diagrams, routing lines, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, split-screen composition, sci-fi lighting, or glossy CGI look.
